# Integration Checklist: TACD-002 Capability Discovery Directive and Agent Updates

**Purpose**: Validate integration-quality requirements for Claude/Codex guidance parity, generated payload refresh, runtime metadata boundaries, and directive pointer resolution.
**Created**: 2026-06-18
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Claude And Codex Runtime Parity

- [x] CHK001 Are all six active Claude Markdown agents and six matching Codex TOML agents included in the scoped runtime guidance set? [Resolved, Spec §Clarifications, Plan §Declared File Operations, Contract §Scope]
- [x] CHK002 Does the spec require Claude and Codex guidance to share the same capability-first semantics through the shared directive or approved equivalent? [Resolved, Spec §FR-003, Spec §FR-004, Spec §SC-002, Contract §Directive Binding]
- [x] CHK003 Are installed Codex TOML compact equivalents allowed only with the exact source-note marker and matching behavior contract? [Resolved, Spec §FR-004, Contract §Directive Binding, Quickstart §Validation Scenario 1]
- [x] CHK004 Does the plan keep TACD-003 prerequisite messaging and TACD-004 deterministic/eval enforcement outside this integration scope? [Resolved, Spec §FR-013, Spec §FR-014, Plan §Constraints]

## Generated Payload Refresh And Drift Control

- [x] CHK005 Is `bash scripts/build-plugin-payloads.sh` identified as the source-derived refresh path for both runtime payload roots? [Resolved, Spec §Clarifications, Plan §Summary, Contract §Generated Payload Refresh]
- [x] CHK006 Does the contract require builder invocation evidence, paired source-to-dist diff review, default-suite verification, and second rebuild idempotence? [Resolved, Contract §Generated Payload Refresh, Quickstart §Validation Scenario 4]
- [x] CHK007 Does the plan explicitly declare generated shared-reference payload copies for `capability-discovery.md`, `consensus-protocol.md`, and `gate-validation.md` under both `dist/claude/speckit-pro/skills/speckit-autopilot/references/` and `dist/codex/speckit-pro/skills/speckit-autopilot/references/`? [Resolved, Plan §Declared File Operations, Builder `scripts/build-plugin-payloads.sh`]
- [x] CHK008 Are source-of-truth and generated payload evidence separated so `dist/**` is refreshed output rather than durable source? [Resolved, Spec §FR-012, Plan §Structure Decision, Preset speckit-pro-reviewability]

## Runtime And Dependency Metadata Boundaries

- [x] CHK009 Are concrete runtime/dependency IDs preserved only as metadata or historical/provenance evidence rather than active preferred-tool behavior? [Resolved, Spec §FR-010, Spec §FR-011, Contract §Metadata Preservation]
- [x] CHK010 Does the PR packet contract require a preserved-ID table with file, field, classification, and behavior-scan result? [Resolved, Spec §PR Review Packet Requirements, Data Model §Schema Metadata ID, Contract §Metadata Preservation]
- [x] CHK011 Are behavior surfaces separated from metadata surfaces, including agent body text, Codex TOML developer instructions, and shared reference prose? [Resolved, Data Model §Runtime Guidance Surface, Contract §Metadata Preservation]

## Directive Pointer Resolution

- [x] CHK012 Is the shared directive path specified exactly as `speckit-pro/skills/speckit-autopilot/references/capability-discovery.md`? [Resolved, Spec §Clarifications, Plan §Summary, Contract §Directive Binding]

## Integration Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK015 Do validation scenarios cover source guidance, evidence wording, metadata classification, generated payload refresh, default suite verification, and scope guard? [Resolved, Quickstart §Validation Scenarios]
